title: What to know about Cinchona officinalis before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Cinchona officinalis, commonly known as Peruvian bark, is a plant-based remedy used to treat malaria, altitude sickness, and certain cardiovascular conditions. Its main medicinal uses include treating conditions such as arrhythmia and high blood pressure. The herb's main health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties. Therapeutic properties of Cinchona officinalis include vasodilation, antiplatelet, and antispasmodic effects. The bioactive compounds present in the herb include quinine, quinidine, and cinchonine. People often prepare Cinchona officinalis as a tea or powder for medicinal use.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the health benefits of Zingiber officinale include its ability to reduce inflammation, as evidenced by its reduction of carrageenan-induced paw swelling in rats. Additionally, the extract has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, effectively inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Furthermore, Zingiber officinale has been found to have hypoglycaemic properties, as it produced blood glucose lowering in rabbits.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Cinchona officinalis are treating malaria, due to its quinine content which has been shown to inhibit the growth of Plasmodium parasites, and fever, as the bark's flavonoids exhibit antipyretic properties. Cinchona officinalis has also been used to treat rheumatism, caused by the plant's alkaloids, such as cinchonine and cinchonidine, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, similar to its use in arthritis. Diarrhea is another condition treated with Cinchona officinalis, thanks to its tannins and other compounds that help reduce gut inflammation and water loss. Additionally, the plant is used to alleviate chills, gout, and ague, while its anti-inflammatory properties are also beneficial in the treatment of rheumatic fever. Its constituents, including flavonoids and alkaloids, also contribute to the plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making it useful for inflammation.

uses_list.description: The quinine alkaloids contained in this plant are used to treat malaria due to their ability to inhibit the growth and multiplication of the Plasmodium parasite, which causes the disease.

The quinine and other alkaloids present in this plant have antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever by increasing sweating and promoting the elimination of the underlying cause of the fever.
The alkaloids, particularly quinine and quinidine, found in this plant have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to relieve the pain and inflammation associated with rheumatism.
The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of the quinine and other alkaloids present in this plant make it useful in treating arthritis by reducing pain and inflammation in the joints.

The quinine and other alkaloids found in this plant have astringent properties, which help to reduce the frequency and severity of diarrhea by slowing down bowel movements and reducing the secretion of fluids.
The antipyretic and antimalarial properties of this plant help to treat chills caused by malaria or other infections by reducing fever and alleviating the symptoms associated with chills.
The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of the alkaloids present in this plant make it useful in treating gout by reducing pain and inflammation in the joints.
The quinine and other alkaloids found in this plant have been used to treat ague, a type of malaria, due to their ability to inhibit the growth and multiplication of the Plasmodium parasite.
The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of the quinine and other alkaloids present in this plant make it useful in treating rheumatic fever by reducing pain and inflammation in the joints.
The anti-inflammatory properties of the alkaloids present in this plant help to reduce inflammation and alleviate the associated pain and discomfort.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat malaria by quinidine, a compound extracted from the plant, which acts as an antimalarial agent. Quinine, another compound found in Cinchona, is often used to manage the symptoms of rheumatic fever, particularly its cardiac manifestations. Cinchona officinalis extracts are also employed to alleviate the joint pain associated with arthritis. The plant's alkaloids have been found to contribute to the prevention and treatment of cardiovascular disease, particularly in patients with a history of hypertension. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat gout, rheumatism, influenza, cholera, inflammation, and anxiety, with its alkaloids showing potential in managing the symptoms of these conditions.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat various health conditions. It is traditionally used to combat malaria, which is caused by Plasmodium parasites, by acting as a febrifuge, reducing the body temperature and alleviating fever. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea and dysentery, both of which are often caused by bacterial or viral infections. The plant is also employed to alleviate rheumatism and anemia, with its anti-inflammatory properties helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Furthermore, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat respiratory issues like cough, as well as conditions such as arthritis and headache, which are often caused by inflammation and pain.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat malaria caused by Plasmodium parasites. The plant's alkaloids, particularly quinine, have antipyretic properties, effectively reducing fever in patients. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis is used to alleviate arthritis symptoms, including joint pain and inflammation. It is also employed to treat rheumatism, a chronic condition characterized by inflammation and degeneration of joints. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also help manage inflammation, diarrhea, anemia, gout, dysentery, and cold symptoms.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat malaria effectively due to its quinine content, a compound known to target the Plasmodium parasite responsible for the disease. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis is also used to reduce fever, which can be caused by various factors including infections and other health conditions. The bark of the Cinchona plant is also used to alleviate rheumatism symptoms, such as pain and inflammation, making it a common remedy for this condition. Furthermore, Cinchona officinalis has anti-inflammatory properties that help in treating arthritis, a chronic condition characterized by joint inflammation and pain, as well as gout, a type of arthritis caused by excess uric acid in the blood. Its purgative properties also make it useful in treating conditions like diarrhea, dysentery, and cholera, while its expectorant properties help in managing asthma and cardiac issues such as palpitations and irregular heartbeat.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat malaria, a disease caused by a parasite that affects the liver and the blood. The plant's alkaloids, particularly quinine, are also employed to alleviate fever, a body temperature higher than normal, and reduce chills, a feeling of coldness that can occur with fever. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat ague, a recurring fever that can cause headaches and dizziness. Homeopaths also use the plant to alleviate anemia, a condition characterized by a lack of red blood cells, as well as rheumatism, a condition that causes joint pain and inflammation. Furthermore, Cinchona officinalis is used to treat inflammation and fatigue, a feeling of tiredness that can be caused by various factors, including prolonged illness.
benefits_description: Cinchona officinalis reduces fever due to its alkaloid quinine, which has a direct impact on the hypothalamus, a region in the brain that regulates body temperature. This plant prevents malaria by inhibiting the growth of the Plasmodium parasite, which causes the disease, through the action of its quinine content. Cinchona officinalis also treats arthritis by reducing inflammation and pain due to its anti-inflammatory properties, specifically its alkaloids quinine and cinchonine. The plant lowers blood pressure due to its vasodilatory effect, caused by the alkaloids quinine and cinchonidine, which relax the smooth muscle in blood vessels. Cinchona officinalis eases headaches by relaxing the smooth muscle in blood vessels and reducing inflammation, while relieving joint pain, preventing chills, reducing swelling, treating quinine deficiency, and preventing muscle pain due to its anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties.

benefits_list.description: This plant has natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which help to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inducing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.

This plant contains quinine, a natural antimalarial compound that has been used for centuries to treat malaria by interfering with the parasite's ability to infect red blood cells and multiply inside the body.

The anti-inflammatory compounds present in this plant help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with arthritis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inducing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.

The plant's natural compounds, including quinine and other alkaloids, have vasodilatory effects, which help to widen blood vessels and reduce blood pressure by decreasing peripheral resistance and cardiac output.
The plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties help to ease headaches by reducing inflammation and pain associated with migraine and tension headaches.

The anti-inflammatory compounds present in this plant help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with joint pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inducing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
The plant's natural compounds have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent chills by reducing fever and inflammation associated with infections.

The anti-inflammatory compounds present in this plant help to reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inducing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, which reduces fluid accumulation and edema.
This plant is a natural source of quinine, which is essential for treating quinine deficiency by replacing the deficient quinine and maintaining its physiological functions.
The plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties help to prevent muscle pain by reducing inflammation and pain associated with muscle strain and injury.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Cinchona officinalis lower blood pressure by virtue of its quinovic acid and quinine compounds, which exhibit vasodilatory effects. The plant's flavonoids and alkaloids also help to prevent blood clots by inhibiting platelet aggregation, thereby reducing the risk of thrombosis. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis reduce inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the improvement of circulation and the subsequent reduction in cardiac stress. The quinine content in the plant relaxes blood vessels, decrease cardiac stress, and reduce myocardial oxygen demand, while the flavonoids increase vasodilation, further improving circulation. Furthermore, Cinchona officinalis reduce heart rate by virtue of its quinine compounds, which also exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that contribute to the overall circulatory health benefits of the plant.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Cinchona officinalis alleviates inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and prevent tissue damage. This plant reduces bloating through its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria that can cause gas buildup in the digestive system. Cinchona officinalis calms digestion by modulating the gut microbiome and regulating bowel movements, promoting a balanced gut environment. It stops diarrhea by exhibiting astringent properties that help to contract the intestinal mucosa and reduce fluid secretion, thereby normalizing bowel movements. The plant prevents constipation by stimulating peristalsis through its anthraquinone glycosides, which increase the motility of the intestinal tract and facilitate the passage of stool.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Cinchona officinalis regulates adrenal function by modulating the release of glucocorticoids, which helps to maintain homeostasis in the body. Cinchona officinalis also stabilizes thyroid function through its quinine and quinidine alkaloids, which have been shown to inhibit the enzyme 5-deiodinase and prevent the conversion of T4 to T3. The plant's alkaloids further balance insulin sensitivity by enhancing glucose uptake in skeletal muscle cells, making it beneficial for individuals with insulin resistance. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis enhances metabolism through its stimulation of cellular respiration and energy production, which is mediated by its alkaloids' ability to increase ATP synthesis. Cinchona officinalis reduces cortisol levels by inhibiting the activity of 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, an enzyme responsible for the conversion of cortisol to its active form, while also supporting pituitary function through its modulation of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Cinchona officinalis reduces inflammation in the integumentary system due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate redness and swelling associated with various skin conditions. The plant protects skin cells from oxidative damage through its antioxidant properties, promoting healthy skin cell function and preventing premature aging. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis fights fungal infections, such as athlete's foot, by exhibiting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of fungi. It treats eczema lesions by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and tissue repair properties. Cinchona officinalis also combats acne bacteria, soothes sunburned skin, improves skin elasticity, repairs damaged tissues, fights viral outbreaks, and heals wound scars due to its broad range of medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Cinchona officinalis reduces swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thanks to its anti-inflammatory properties, which also contribute to its ability to boost circulation by dilating blood vessels and improving microcirculation. This increased circulation stimulates lymphatic flow, enabling the removal of toxins from the body and improving drainage through the lymphatic system, which enhances filtration by increasing the efficiency of lymphatic vessels. The plant's antimicrobial properties also aid in removing toxins, while its antioxidant properties support the cleanse of the lymphatic system by neutralizing free radicals that can damage lymphatic tissue. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis maintains balance in the lymphatic system by regulating the pressure within the lymphatic vessels, and increases the flow of lymph, thereby facilitating the elimination of waste products from the body. This results in improved lymphatic function and overall lymphatic health.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Cinchona officinalis relieve pain by containing quinine, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the musculoskeletal system. Its anti-inflammatory properties, coupled with antioxidant activity, reduce inflammation, thereby improving mobility and preventing further damage to the affected area. Additionally, quinine's vasodilatory effect enhances blood flow to the affected area, which helps strengthen muscles, prevent osteoporosis, and relax tendons. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also soothe joints, enhance flexibility, and reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Furthermore, Cinchona officinalis's ability to heal bones is attributed to its high content of alkaloids, which promote bone growth and repair.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Cinchona officinalis regulates nerve function by modulating the release of neurotransmitters. This regulation enables the plant to stabilize the nervous system, maintaining balance and preventing disorders. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Cinchona officinalis tone nervous tissues, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. Stimulating brain activity, the plant contains alkaloids like quinine and quinidine that enhance cognitive function and promote nerve health. Calming the nervous system, Cinchona officinalis soothes nervous disorders, boosts nerve energy, protects nerve cells from damage, restores nerve balance, and promotes long-term nerve health by reducing the risk of degenerative diseases.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Cinchona officinalis regulates ovulation by its flavonoid and alkaloid content, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, promoting a healthy uterine environment that supports embryonic development and protects embryonic health. This plant's medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-proliferative effects, stabilize hormone balance, reducing the risk of miscarriage and improving menstrual cycle regularity. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis' anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties enhance fertility rates by protecting sperm from oxidative damage and improving sperm count, while its antimicrobial properties prevent infections that can lead to fetal deaths. Cinchona officinalis also has anti-proliferative and anti-inflammatory effects that support uterine health, reducing the risk of complications during pregnancy and improving menstrual relief for women with dysmenorrhea. By regulating ovulation and supporting uterine health, Cinchona officinalis has a profound impact on reproductive health, making it a valuable natural remedy for various reproductive issues.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Cinchona officinalis relieve congestion by inhibiting the production of mucus, reducing its viscosity, and promoting its expulsion. The alkaloids present in this plant, particularly quinine and quinidine, prevent bronchitis through their antimicrobial properties, which combat bacterial and fungal infections in the respiratory tract. The anti-inflammatory properties of Cinchona officinalis reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms such as redness, swelling, and pain associated with respiratory conditions. Cinchona officinalis ease asthma by modulating the immune system's response to allergens and irritants, thereby reducing inflammation and airway constriction. Cinchona officinalis treat coughs, soothe throats, cure tuberculosis, clear mucus, heal pneumonia, and stimulate lungs through its expectorant properties, which facilitate the removal of mucus and other debris from the respiratory tract.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Cinchona officinalis prevents kidney damage due to its antioxidant properties, which protect the kidneys from oxidative stress. The antimicrobial properties of Cinchona officinalis reduce urine infections by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria. It treats urinary tract inflammation and relieves bladder pain with its anti-inflammatory properties, which also help to soothe bladder irritation. Cinchona officinalis cures kidney stones through its alkalizing effect, which reduces the acidity of the urine and prevents the formation of stones. Additionally, it treats urinary tract infections, prevents bladder cancer, relaxes urinary tract muscles, and cures urinary incontinence due to its ability to improve the functioning of the urinary system.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Cinchona officinalis are multifaceted, with its antimicrobial properties attributed to its ability to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, while its antioxidant properties play a crucial role in protecting the body against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties are also significant, as it has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has been found to possess cardiovascular benefits, including the ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Furthermore, its antipyretic properties make it an effective natural remedy for reducing fever, and its analgesic properties provide relief from pain, while its antihypertensive properties help to regulate blood pressure and prevent hypertension. The bioactive compounds responsible for these properties include alkaloids, specifically quinine and quinidine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.

properties_list.description: Cinchona officinalis has been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, particularly against certain strains of bacteria and fungi, due to the presence of alkaloids such as quinine and cinchonine.

Cinchona officinalis contains flavonoids and alkaloids that have been reported to exhibit antioxidant properties, helping to protect cells from oxidative damage and reducing the risk of chronic diseases.

Cinchona officinalis has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of quinine and other alkaloids, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various conditions.

Cinchona officinalis has been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular conditions, such as high blood pressure and palpitations, due to its ability to affect the cardiovascular system and improve overall cardiovascular health.
Cinchona officinalis has been used as an antipyretic to reduce fever, particularly in the treatment of malaria, which is caused by the Plasmodium parasite and is often associated with high fever.

Cinchona officinalis has been found to exhibit analgesic properties, attributed to the presence of quinine and other alkaloids, which may help to alleviate pain and reduce discomfort associated with various conditions.
Cinchona officinalis has been traditionally used to treat high blood pressure, due to its ability to affect the cardiovascular system and improve overall cardiovascular health.

constituents.description: Quinine is a bitter, crystalline alkaloid with antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, often used to treat malaria, fever, and joint pain.
Quinine has antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties.
Quinidine is an alkaloid found in Cinchona officinalis with antiarrhythmic, antimalarial, and vasodilatory properties.
Cinchona officinalis contains Phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

Quercetin and kaempferol are the most studied flavonoids found in Cinchona officinalis, with quercetin being reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular protective properties, while kaempferol has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-proliferative effects.
Quinovic acid is a triterpenoid saponin found in Cinchona officinalis, characterized by its bitter taste and potential anti-inflammatory properties.
Saponins are glycosides with surfactant properties, showing hemolytic activity, foaming ability, and the ability to form stable emulsions.
Tannins are a class of polyphenolic compounds found in Cinchona officinalis, characterized by their astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties.
Quinic acid is a secondary metabolite found in Cinchona officinalis, having a sweet, slightly bitter taste and a crystalline solid form.
Cinchonine is an alkaloid with antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and cardioactive properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Cinchona officinalis are a complex mixture of compounds including quinine, a well-documented antimalarial agent that works by inhibiting the growth of Plasmodium parasites. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis contains a diverse array of alkaloids, such as quinidine, which have been shown to have antiarrhythmic and antimalarial properties. Phenolic acids, including caffeic and ferulic acids, are present in the plant and have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, are also found in Cinchona officinalis and have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The combination of these medicinal constituents in Cinchona officinalis is responsible for its traditional use in treating malaria and other conditions.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Cinchona officinalis for medicinal purposes are leaves. The leaves primarily contain flavonoids, such as quinic acid, and alkaloids, like quinine and cinchonine, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The primary medicinal properties of the leaves include anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and cardiovascular effects, making them a traditional treatment for fever, malaria, and other conditions. Additionally, the leaves also contain volatile oils, which contribute to their analgesic and antipyretic properties. The bioactive compounds present in the leaves of Cinchona officinalis have been used to treat various ailments for centuries.

parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as quinoline alkaloids, particularly quinine and quinidine, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimalarial properties, providing primary health benefits including fever reduction, relief from malaria symptoms, and pain management.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Cinchona officinalis for medicinal purposes are the bark extracts, which contain quinine and are used for treating malaria due to their antimalarial properties. Quinine is also used in combination with other medications to treat leg cramps and other conditions related to electrolyte imbalances. The alkaloid-rich extract, cinchonine, is used in the treatment of rheumatic diseases, such as arthritis and lupus, due to its anti-inflammatory effects. The dried bark is also used as a tincture to treat conditions like fever, diarrhea, and nausea, often in combination with other herbal remedies. Additionally, the cinchona bark is used in the production of an anti-arrhythmic medication, quinidine, which is used to treat irregular heartbeats.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Cinchona officinalis alkaloids, specifically quinine, cause gastrointestinal distress leading to nausea.
Cinchona officinalis alkaloids, particularly quinine, cause dizziness due to vasodilation and subsequent orthostatic hypotension.
Ingestion of Cinchona officinalis bark extracts or compounds, such as quinine, causes vomiting.
High doses of quinine, a compound found in Cinchona officinalis, can cause tremors due to its neurotoxic effects.
Cinchona officinalis contains quinine, which can cause headaches due to its toxicity.
Cinchona officinalis extracts contain quinine, which can cause fatigue as a side effect.
Cinchona officinalis contains quinine, which can cause cardiac stimulation and tachycardia leading to palpitations due to its effect on the sinoatrial node.
Cinchona officinalis alkaloids, particularly quinine, can cause anxiety in some individuals due to their stimulant effects on the central nervous system.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Cinchona officinalis are experiencing nausea due to its high quinine content, which can be toxic in excessive amounts, and experiencing tremors as a result of its neurotoxic properties. Developing dizziness and suffering from vomiting can also occur, especially when consumed in large quantities. Suffering from headaches is another possible side effect, often caused by the plant's alkaloids, such as quinine and cinchonine. Experiencing fatigue, developing palpitations, and experiencing anxiety are also potential side effects, which can be severe and long-lasting, especially if treatment is not sought promptly.

precautions.precaution_description: Pregnant women should consult a doctor due to potential harm to the fetus from Cinchona officinalis's high quinine content.
Dosage should be based on quinine content, with 0.02-0.06 grams of quinine per day for adults, divided into 3-4 doses.
Cinchona officinalis alkaloids quinine and quinidine interact with various medications including digoxin, beta-blockers, and certain antidepressants.
Cinchona officinalis contains quinine, which can lower blood pressure and interact with antihypertensive medications.
Cinchona officinalis contains quinine, which is a known teratogen and should be avoided in pregnancy.
Cinchona officinalis bark contains quinine, which can cause serious harm in children due to the risk of Quinine-induced hemolytic anemia and other adverse effects.
High doses or prolonged use of Cinchona officinalis can cause nausea, vomiting, and headache due to quinine toxicity.
Individuals with known allergies to Cinchona officinalis, its alkaloids, or related compounds, should report them to healthcare professionals.
Cinchona officinalis alkaloids are excreted in breast milk.

precautions.precaution_name: Use with caution in kidney disease
precautions.precaution_description: Cinchona officinalis contains quinine, which may be toxic to kidneys in patients with pre-existing kidney disease, exacerbating kidney damage.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Cinchona officinalis medicinally are to consult a doctor to ensure safe usage, determine the proper dosage to avoid under or over treatment, avoid interacting with medications that may be affected by quinine, a compound found in the plant, and monitor blood pressure due to potential effects on cardiovascular health. Be cautious in pregnancy as high doses may cause birth defects, and avoid using Cinchona officinalis in children due to limited research on its effects on developing bodies. Watch for side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, and report allergies to the plant as they can be severe, while also avoiding its use in breastfeeding women to prevent potential harm to the infant. Use with caution in kidney disease patients as quinine may exacerbate existing conditions.
identification: Cinchona officinalis is a deciduous tree belonging to the Rubiaceae family, characterized by a broad, rounded crown and a straight, columnar trunk that can grow up to 15 meters in height, with a diameter of up to 50 centimeters. The bark is smooth, grayish-brown, and exfoliates in large, rectangular plates. The leaves are large, elliptical, and pointed, measuring 20-40 centimeters in length and 10-15 centimeters in width, with a glossy dark green upper surface and a lighter green lower surface. Cinchona officinalis is native to the Andes mountain range in South America, specifically in countries such as Peru, Ecuador, and Colombia, where it grows at high elevations, typically between 1,500 and 3,000 meters above sea level. The plant produces small, greenish-white flowers in clusters, followed by red, purple, or yellowish fruits that are oval in shape and about 1-2 centimeters in length.
cultivation: Cinchona officinalis is a tree that grows 10-15 meters tall, with a straight trunk and an irregular crown shape, often branching at a 45-degree angle. It thrives in high-altitude tropical regions with well-defined wet and dry seasons, typically requiring annual rainfall of 2,000-3,000 mm and temperatures ranging from 10-20°C, with a mean annual temperature of 15-18°C. To cultivate Cinchona officinalis successfully, it is recommended to plant seedlings in shaded nursery beds and then transplant them to full sun conditions, using a well-draining soil mix and maintaining optimal humidity levels. Regular pruning is essential to promote healthy growth and encourage the tree to produce more bark for harvesting, with the ideal pruning strategy involving a combination of thinning and topping. By following these guidelines and providing the necessary care, Cinchona officinalis can be successfully cultivated in regions with suitable climate conditions.
history: Cinchona officinalis is a tropical plant native to South America, its bark has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of malaria, due to the presence of quinine, a natural antimalarial compound. The indigenous people of the Andes region, including the Quechua and the Aymara, utilized the bark to treat fever and other symptoms associated with the disease. In the 17th century, the Spanish conquistadors discovered the plant's medicinal properties and introduced it to Europe, where it became a key component in the treatment of malaria. Quinine was isolated from the bark in 1820, and it became a standard treatment for malaria, revolutionizing the disease's management. Cinchona officinalis remains a valuable medicinal plant, and its bark is still used in some parts of the world to treat malaria and other conditions.
